gaps.snippet: nodule std.spore.deploy;
gaps.reason: derived nodule path `std.spore.deploy` has segment(s) [spore] colliding with a Mycelium reserved word — emitting them verbatim would fail to parse (`nodule std.spore.deploy;` tokenizes the colliding word as a keyword, not an identifier); each colliding segment is escaped with the `_kw` suffix (now `std.spore_kw.deploy`) rather than dropped, so distinct source files whose only differing segment is a reserved word (e.g. `l1.fuse` vs `l1.nodule`) stay distinguishable instead of both collapsing onto the same nodule path (G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Where to deploy a spore."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is a seam type: the v0 stub supports [`DeployTarget::InMemory`] and"] # [doc = " [`DeployTarget::Local`]. Future variants (remote, WASM, MLIR native) will extend this without"] # [doc = " breaking the never-silent contract — every new target must still go through `germinate` and"] # [doc = " must fail closed on ambiguous / missing input."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic target selection — no hidden defaults)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: target validation is the caller's responsibility; `germinate` errors on ambiguity"] # [doc = " # Effects: none (the type itself has no effects; `germinate` carries the `io` effect)"] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum DeployTarget { # [doc = " Deploy the spore entirely in memory — no filesystem or network effect."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Used for testing the germination seam without requiring a filesystem target. The spore's"] # [doc = " identity is checked and the no-opaque-lowering check runs; no bytes are written."] InMemory , # [doc = " Deploy to a local filesystem path."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The path is validated during `germinate`; a missing/empty path becomes"] # [doc = " [`DeployError::MissingInput`] before any other work is done."] Local { # [doc = " Absolute or relative path for the deployed unit. Must not be empty."] path : String , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `DeployTarget` variant `Local` named field(s) `path` emitted positionally as `Local(Bytes)` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " What was verified during a successful deploy (VR-4 / C4 / ADR-003)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Carried inside [`DeployResult::Deployed`] to give callers an inspectable, EXPLAIN-able record"] # [doc = " of which invariants were checked. Both fields must be `true` for a deploy to succeed — a false"] # [doc = " value is an internal logic error (the `germinate` function refuses before returning `Deployed`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Honesty note"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `no_opaque_lowering` field is `Declared` strength: it is a structural assertion (no opaque"] # [doc = " lowering step present in the pipeline), not a proof. The MLIR toolchain verification is"] # [doc = " out-of-scope until the native toolchain is integrated."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct DeployVerification { # [doc = " Whether the content hash was verified as canonical (deterministic BLAKE3 — `Exact`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `true` iff the spore's declared identity matched the recomputed hash at deploy time (C4 /"] # [doc = " ADR-003). A `false` here is never returned — `germinate` refuses with `HashMismatch` first."] pub content_hash_canonical : bool , # [doc = " Whether no opaque lowering step was detected between input and deployed unit (VR-4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Declared` strength: a structural assertion (no opaque step registered in the pipeline)."] # [doc = " Full proof requires the MLIR toolchain. A `false` here is never returned — `germinate`"] # [doc = " refuses with `OpaqueStepDetected` first."] pub no_opaque_lowering : bool , }
gaps.reason: struct `DeployVerification` named field(s) `content_hash_canonical, no_opaque_lowering` emitted positionally as `DeployVerification(Bool, Bool)` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped (matches `lib/std/*.myc` hand-ports)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The outcome of a germination attempt (ADR-013 native path — M-620)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " On success: `Deployed { spore_id, verification }` — carries the deployed spore's content-hash"] # [doc = " identity and a [`DeployVerification`] record of what was checked."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " On failure: `Failed(DeployError)` — **never silent** (G2 / VR-5). The `Failed` variant"] # [doc = " carries an explicit, named [`DeployError`]; there is no \"partial deploy\" or \"best-effort\""] # [doc = " variant."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Note: callers should prefer the `Result<DeployResult, DeployError>` return of [`germinate`]"] # [doc = " over constructing this directly. The `Deployed` variant is only reachable when all checks pass."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum DeployResult { # [doc = " The spore was deployed successfully and all checks passed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `spore_id` is the content-hash identity of the deployed unit (C4 / ADR-003)."] # [doc = " `verification` is the EXPLAIN-able record of what was verified (VR-4)."] Deployed { # [doc = " The content-hash identity of the deployed unit."] spore_id : ContentHash , # [doc = " The verification record (VR-4 / C4)."] verification : DeployVerification , } , # [doc = " The deploy failed with an explicit, named error — never a silent best-effort outcome."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Note: in practice `germinate` returns `Err(DeployError)` directly; this variant exists for"] # [doc = " completeness and for callers that batch deploy results."] Failed (DeployError) , }
gaps.reason: enum `DeployResult` has a named-field variant referencing a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An explicit deploy error — never a silent fallback (G2 / VR-4 / VR-5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Each variant names the exact condition that triggered it. The conditions are mutually"] # [doc = " exclusive in the v0 stub; `germinate` checks them in order: MissingInput → AmbiguousInput →"] # [doc = " HashMismatch → OpaqueStepDetected → success."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Condition triggers (exact — never guessed or silently recovered)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - [`MissingInput`](DeployError::MissingInput): required input absent (e.g. empty path for"] # [doc = "   `Local` target, or a target-required field is `None`)."] # [doc = " - [`AmbiguousInput`](DeployError::AmbiguousInput): more than one candidate matches; the caller"] # [doc = "   must disambiguate. All candidates are listed so the caller can surface them (G11)."] # [doc = " - [`HashMismatch`](DeployError::HashMismatch): the declared spore identity did not match the"] # [doc = "   recomputed hash at deploy time (C4 / ADR-003 violation). Both hashes are carried."] # [doc = " - [`OpaqueStepDetected`](DeployError::OpaqueStepDetected): an opaque lowering step was"] # [doc = "   detected in the native deploy pipeline (VR-4 violation). The step name is carried."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum DeployError { # [doc = " A required deploy input is absent."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Triggered when: the deploy target has no path (empty `Local::path`), or a required"] # [doc = " field is `None`. Never guessed, never silently defaulted (G2)."] MissingInput , # [doc = " More than one candidate matches the deploy specification; the caller must disambiguate."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Carries all candidates so the caller can surface them in the diagnostic (G11). Returning"] # [doc = " a silent guess is strictly forbidden (G2)."] AmbiguousInput { # [doc = " All candidates that matched — at least two (otherwise this is `MissingInput`)."] candidates : Vec < String > , } , # [doc = " The spore's declared content-hash identity did not match the recomputed hash at deploy"] # [doc = " time (C4 / ADR-003 violation). Deploy is aborted; no partial unit is written."] HashMismatch { # [doc = " The hash the spore claimed as its identity."] expected : String , # [doc = " The hash recomputed at deploy time."] actual : String , } , # [doc = " An opaque lowering step was detected between the input spore and the deployed unit (VR-4"] # [doc = " violation). The deploy is aborted; the step name is carried for the diagnostic."] OpaqueStepDetected { # [doc = " The name of the detected opaque step (e.g. `\"mlir-lower-opaque\"`, `\"jit-compile\"`)."] step : String , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `DeployError` variant `AmbiguousInput` named field(s) `candidates` emitted positionally as `AmbiguousInput(Vec[Bytes])` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped

gaps.reason: enum `DeployError` derive(Eq): covered by co-occurring PartialEq → `fn eq_DeployError` (product-row collision policy; DN-128 / ONESHOT C2) — not a second emission

gaps.snippet:
impl std :: fmt :: Display for DeployError { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut std :: fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> std :: fmt :: Result { match self { DeployError :: MissingInput => write ! (f , "deploy-error: required input is absent — no guessed default (G2 / ADR-013)") , DeployError :: AmbiguousInput { candidates } => write ! (f , "deploy-error: ambiguous input — {} candidates match; disambiguate before deploying \
                 (G2 / ADR-013): [{}]" , candidates . len () , candidates . join (", ")) , DeployError :: HashMismatch { expected , actual } => write ! (f , "deploy-error: content-hash mismatch — declared {} but recomputed {} \
                 (C4 / ADR-003 violation; deploy aborted, no partial unit written — G2)" , expected , actual) , DeployError :: OpaqueStepDetected { step } => write ! (f , "deploy-error: opaque lowering step detected: {:?} \
                 (VR-4 violation; no opaque step is permitted between input and deployed unit)" , step) , } } }
gaps.reason: trait-impl of non-prelude trait `Display` — no ambient trait definition and synthetic trait emission is refused (DN-34 §8.8 / DN-122 MVP); gapped the whole impl so the residual cannot file-poison myc-check (G2; express gap-close 2026-07-16)

gaps.snippet: mycelium_std_core :: impl_std_error ! (DeployError) ;
gaps.reason: item-position macro invocation — no macro system in this grammar

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Deploy a [`SporeUnit`] to a [`DeployTarget`] — the ADR-013 native germination entry point."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The v0 stub implements the structural invariant checks without a full runtime:"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " 1. **Missing-input check**: if the target requires a path (e.g. [`DeployTarget::Local`]) and"] # [doc = "    it is empty, return `Err(DeployError::MissingInput)`."] # [doc = " 2. **Ambiguous-input check**: if the target description resolves to more than one candidate,"] # [doc = "    return `Err(DeployError::AmbiguousInput { candidates })` listing all of them. (In this stub,"] # [doc = "    candidates are derived from the target descriptor and the spore surface list.)"] # [doc = " 3. **Hash-canonicality check**: recompute the spore's identity and compare to the declared"] # [doc = "    hash (ADR-003 / C4). If they diverge, return `Err(DeployError::HashMismatch { expected,"] # [doc = "    actual })` naming both hashes. Never silently accept a mismatch."] # [doc = " 4. **No-opaque-lowering check**: assert no opaque lowering step is present in the pipeline"] # [doc = "    (VR-4). In the v0 stub, the known opaque steps are checked by name. If one is present,"] # [doc = "    return `Err(DeployError::OpaqueStepDetected { step })` naming it."] # [doc = " 5. **Success**: all checks pass → `Ok(DeployResult::Deployed { spore_id, verification })`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Empirical`"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The VR-4 check is structural (no opaque step in the stub pipeline) but the full native deploy"] # [doc = " path is not yet proven end-to-end — the MLIR toolchain integration is Phase-6-gated. No tag"] # [doc = " upgrade without a checked basis (VR-5). The content-hash check is `Exact`; the overall op tag"] # [doc = " is bounded by the weakest component (`Declared` for the opaque-step check; `Empirical` for the"] # [doc = " full path)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - `Err(DeployError::MissingInput)` — required input absent"] # [doc = " - `Err(DeployError::AmbiguousInput { candidates })` — more than one candidate"] # [doc = " - `Err(DeployError::HashMismatch { expected, actual })` — hash mismatch"] # [doc = " - `Err(DeployError::OpaqueStepDetected { step })` — VR-4 violation"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: `io` (may write to filesystem for `Local` target; `InMemory` has no effects)"] pub fn germinate (spore : & SporeUnit , target : & DeployTarget) -> Result < DeployResult , DeployError > { match target { DeployTarget :: Local { path } if path . is_empty () => { return Err (DeployError :: MissingInput) ; } _ => { } } let surface = spore . raw () . surface . clone () ; if let DeployTarget :: InMemory = target { if surface . len () > 1 { return Err (DeployError :: AmbiguousInput { candidates : surface , }) ; } } if let Err (e) = spore . verify () { let (expected , actual) = match e { crate :: spore_ops :: SporeErr :: HashMismatch { expected , found } => { (expected . as_str () . to_owned () , found . as_str () . to_owned ()) } other => { let _ = other ; return Err (DeployError :: MissingInput) ; } } ; return Err (DeployError :: HashMismatch { expected , actual }) ; } let opaque_step = detect_opaque_step (target) ; if let Some (step) = opaque_step { return Err (DeployError :: OpaqueStepDetected { step }) ; } let spore_id = spore . identity () . clone () ; let verification = DeployVerification { content_hash_canonical : true , no_opaque_lowering : true , } ; Ok (DeployResult :: Deployed { spore_id , verification , }) }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " The EXPLAIN of a germination outcome — VR-4 / SC-3 / C3 / G11."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns a human-readable, deterministic string describing:"] # [doc = " - Whether the deploy succeeded or failed,"] # [doc = " - The spore identity (or the error),"] # [doc = " - What was checked (content-hash canonical, no-opaque-lowering),"] # [doc = " - The VR-4 opaque-lowering result explicitly."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The string always contains \"hash\" and \"opaque\" (or \"lowering\") so VR-4 is always surfaced"] # [doc = " in the EXPLAIN output — no silent omission."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic; a total pure function of the result)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn explain_deploy (result : & DeployResult) -> String { match result { DeployResult :: Deployed { spore_id , verification , } => { format ! ("deploy-result: Deployed\n\
                 spore-id (content-hash): {id}\n\
                 content_hash_canonical: {hash_ok} (Exact — BLAKE3 deterministic; C4/ADR-003)\n\
                 no_opaque_lowering: {opaque_ok} (Declared — structural assertion; VR-4)\n\
                 outcome: all invariants checked; no opaque lowering step detected in pipeline" , id = spore_id . as_str () , hash_ok = verification . content_hash_canonical , opaque_ok = verification . no_opaque_lowering ,) } DeployResult :: Failed (err) => { format ! ("deploy-result: Failed\n\
                 error: {err}\n\
                 content_hash_canonical: not verified (deploy aborted before hash check, \
                 or hash mismatch detected — C4/ADR-003)\n\
                 no_opaque_lowering: not verified (deploy aborted before opaque-lowering check; \
                 VR-4 assertion was not reached or triggered the refusal)" ,) } } }
gaps.reason: struct-variant pattern `Deployed { .. }` names a constructor with no confirmed in-file layout -- resolved only for an emitted, in-file struct (or, once DN-132's SS5.1 variant-aware `StructLayout` population lands, an in-file enum's named-field variant); never a guessed field-index arity (DN-132 SS5.1/OQ-4, VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet:
# [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; use crate :: spore_ops :: SporeUnit ; use mycelium_core :: { meta :: { Meta , Provenance } , repr :: Repr , value :: { Payload , Value } , } ; # [doc = " Construct a well-formed single-value SporeUnit with exactly one surface symbol."] fn single_surface_spore () -> SporeUnit { let v = Value :: new (Repr :: Binary { width : 8 } , Payload :: Bits (vec ! [true , false , true , true , false , false , true , false]) , Meta :: exact (Provenance :: Root) ,) . expect ("well-formed byte value") ; SporeUnit :: from_value (& v , None) . expect ("from_value always Ok for well-formed value") } # [doc = " Construct a SporeUnit whose raw Spore has multiple surface exports, for ambiguous-input testing."] # [doc = " We use a project build to get a multi-export surface."] fn multi_surface_spore () -> SporeUnit { use mycelium_proj :: parse_manifest ; use std :: io :: Write as IoWrite ; let m_src = "[project]
name="multi"
kind="phylum"
\
                     [surface]
exports=["alpha","beta"]
" ; let dir = { let dir = std :: env :: temp_dir () . join (format ! ("myc-deploy-multi-{}" , std :: time :: SystemTime :: now () . duration_since (std :: time :: UNIX_EPOCH) . unwrap () . as_nanos ())) ; std :: fs :: create_dir_all (& dir) . unwrap () ; std :: fs :: write (dir . join ("mycelium-proj.toml") , m_src) . unwrap () ; for (rel , content) in & [("alpha.myc" , "nodule alpha
fn f() -> Binary{8} = 0b0
") , ("beta.myc" , "nodule beta
fn g() -> Binary{8} = 0b1
") ,] { let p = dir . join (rel) ; let mut f = std :: fs :: File :: create (p) . unwrap () ; f . write_all (content . as_bytes ()) . unwrap () ; } dir } ; let m = parse_manifest (m_src) . unwrap () ; SporeUnit :: from_manifest (& m , & dir) . expect ("multi-surface project builds ok") } # [doc = " `germinate` with a `Local` target whose path is empty returns `Err(MissingInput)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Mutant witness"] # [doc = " Returning `Ok(...)` here instead of `Err(MissingInput)` makes this test fail."] # [test] fn test_germinate_missing_input () { let spore = single_surface_spore () ; let target = DeployTarget :: Local { path : String :: new () , } ; let result = germinate (& spore , & target) ; assert ! (matches ! (result , Err (DeployError :: MissingInput)) , "empty Local path must return Err(MissingInput), got: {result:?}") ; match result { Err (DeployError :: MissingInput) => { } other => panic ! ("expected Err(DeployError::MissingInput), got {other:?}") , } } # [doc = " `germinate` with an `InMemory` target and a spore with two surface exports returns"] # [doc = " `Err(AmbiguousInput { candidates })` naming both candidates."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Mutant witness"] # [doc = " Returning `Ok(...)` here instead of `Err(AmbiguousInput)` makes this test fail."] # [test] fn test_germinate_ambiguous_input () { let spore = multi_surface_spore () ; let target = DeployTarget :: InMemory ; let result = germinate (& spore , & target) ; match result { Err (DeployError :: AmbiguousInput { ref candidates }) => { assert ! (candidates . contains (& "alpha" . to_owned ()) , "candidates must include 'alpha': {candidates:?}") ; assert ! (candidates . contains (& "beta" . to_owned ()) , "candidates must include 'beta': {candidates:?}") ; assert_eq ! (candidates . len () , 2 , "must have exactly 2 candidates: {candidates:?}") ; } other => panic ! ("expected Err(DeployError::AmbiguousInput {{ candidates: [alpha, beta] }}), \
                 got {other:?}") , } } # [doc = " `germinate` with a spore that has a tampered identity returns"] # [doc = " `Err(HashMismatch { expected, actual })` naming both hashes."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Mutant witness"] # [doc = " Returning `Ok(...)` here instead of `Err(HashMismatch)` violates C4/ADR-003."] # [test] fn test_germinate_hash_mismatch () { use mycelium_core :: ContentHash ; let spore = single_surface_spore () ; let real_id = spore . identity () . clone () ; let fake_id = ContentHash :: from_parts ("blake3" , "deadbeef00000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ,) . unwrap () ; let tampered = spore . with_tampered_id (fake_id . clone ()) ; let target = DeployTarget :: Local { path : "/tmp/test-deploy-mismatch" . to_owned () , } ; let result = germinate (& tampered , & target) ; match result { Err (DeployError :: HashMismatch { ref expected , ref actual , }) => { assert_eq ! (expected , fake_id . as_str () , "expected field must be the declared (tampered) id") ; assert_eq ! (actual , real_id . as_str () , "actual field must be the recomputed real hash") ; } other => panic ! ("expected Err(DeployError::HashMismatch {{ .. }}), got {other:?}") , } } # [doc = " `germinate` when `detect_opaque_step` would return a step name returns"] # [doc = " `Err(OpaqueStepDetected { step })` naming the step."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Since the v0 stub always returns `None` from `detect_opaque_step`, we test the error"] # [doc = " path by verifying the error type/Display directly."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Mutant witness"] # [doc = " Returning `Ok(...)` instead of `Err(OpaqueStepDetected)` violates VR-4."] # [test] fn test_germinate_opaque_step () { let step_name = "mlir-lower-opaque" ; let err = DeployError :: OpaqueStepDetected { step : step_name . to_owned () , } ; match & err { DeployError :: OpaqueStepDetected { step } => { assert_eq ! (step , step_name , "step field must be the name of the detected step") ; } other => panic ! ("expected DeployError::OpaqueStepDetected {{ step: {step_name:?} }}, got {other:?}") , } let msg = format ! ("{err}") ; assert ! (msg . contains ("opaque") || msg . contains ("VR-4") , "OpaqueStepDetected display must mention 'opaque' or 'VR-4': {msg}") ; assert ! (msg . contains (step_name) , "OpaqueStepDetected display must name the step: {msg}") ; let result = DeployResult :: Failed (err . clone ()) ; let explain = explain_deploy (& result) ; assert ! (explain . contains ("opaque") || explain . contains ("lowering") , "explain_deploy of OpaqueStepDetected must mention opaque/lowering: {explain}") ; } # [doc = " `germinate` with a well-formed single-surface spore and a `Local` target returns"] # [doc = " `Ok(DeployResult::Deployed { spore_id, verification })` with both verification flags `true`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Mutant witness"] # [doc = " Returning `Err(...)` here instead of `Ok(Deployed { .. })` makes this test fail."] # [test] fn test_germinate_success () { let spore = single_surface_spore () ; let expected_id = spore . identity () . clone () ; let target = DeployTarget :: Local { path : "/tmp/test-spore-deploy-success" . to_owned () , } ; let result = germinate (& spore , & target) . expect ("well-formed spore must deploy without error") ; match result { DeployResult :: Deployed { spore_id , verification , } => { assert_eq ! (spore_id , expected_id , "deployed spore_id must equal the spore's declared identity") ; assert ! (verification . content_hash_canonical , "content_hash_canonical must be true on success") ; assert ! (verification . no_opaque_lowering , "no_opaque_lowering must be true on success") ; } other => panic ! ("expected DeployResult::Deployed {{ .. }}, got {other:?}") , } } # [doc = " `germinate` with `InMemory` target and a single-surface spore succeeds"] # [doc = " (single surface = unambiguous entry point)."] # [test] fn test_germinate_in_memory_single_surface_success () { let spore = single_surface_spore () ; let target = DeployTarget :: InMemory ; let result = germinate (& spore , & target) ; assert ! (matches ! (result , Ok (DeployResult :: Deployed { .. })) , "InMemory + single-surface must succeed: {result:?}") ; } # [doc = " `explain_deploy` output must contain both "hash" and "opaque" (or "lowering") as"] # [doc = " substrings, proving VR-4 is surfaced in the EXPLAIN output regardless of outcome."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Mutant witness"] # [doc = " Removing "hash" from the output string makes the first assertion fail."] # [doc = " Removing "opaque"/"lowering" from the output string makes the second assertion fail."] # [test] fn test_explain_deploy_covers_vr4 () { let spore = single_surface_spore () ; let spore_id = spore . identity () . clone () ; let success = DeployResult :: Deployed { spore_id , verification : DeployVerification { content_hash_canonical : true , no_opaque_lowering : true , } , } ; let explain_success = explain_deploy (& success) ; assert ! (explain_success . contains ("hash") , "explain_deploy(Deployed) must mention 'hash' (C4/ADR-003): {explain_success}") ; assert ! (explain_success . contains ("opaque") || explain_success . contains ("lowering") , "explain_deploy(Deployed) must mention 'opaque' or 'lowering' (VR-4): {explain_success}") ; let failure = DeployResult :: Failed (DeployError :: OpaqueStepDetected { step : "jit-compile" . to_owned () , }) ; let explain_failure = explain_deploy (& failure) ; assert ! (explain_failure . contains ("hash") , "explain_deploy(Failed) must mention 'hash': {explain_failure}") ; assert ! (explain_failure . contains ("opaque") || explain_failure . contains ("lowering") , "explain_deploy(Failed) must mention 'opaque' or 'lowering' (VR-4): {explain_failure}") ; } # [doc = " `explain_deploy` is deterministic — same result always produces the same string (`Exact`)."] # [test] fn test_explain_deploy_is_deterministic () { let spore = single_surface_spore () ; let spore_id = spore . identity () . clone () ; let result = DeployResult :: Deployed { spore_id , verification : DeployVerification { content_hash_canonical : true , no_opaque_lowering : true , } , } ; assert_eq ! (explain_deploy (& result) , explain_deploy (& result) , "explain_deploy must be deterministic (Exact)") ; } }
gaps.reason: #[cfg(test)] item — out of scope for this PoC's transpilation surface (excluded from the expressible-fraction denominator, but recorded, never silently skipped)
